ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Continued To Dec-28-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Action For Failure To Serve Complaint Within Three Years And Delay In Prosecution
Off Calendar Nov-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT YUKO OYAMA'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff's Action For Failure To Serve Complaint Within Three Years And Delay In Prosecution is granted. Dismissal is mandated by CCP 538.250. None of the tolling provisions in section 583.240 apply. Plaintiff Diane Di Prima has not shown that she made any attempt to investigate the whereabouts of or locate Ms. Oyama until shortly before the three-year limit expired, which precludes a finding of impossibility, impracticability, or futility. (A. Groppe & Sons Glass Co., Inc. v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (1991) 232 Cal.App.3d 220, 224-225.) Ms. Di Prima cannot rely on the efforts of the investigator for Ms. Oyama's insurer to locate Ms. Oyama because Ms. Di Prima must show she took reasonable steps to effectuate service of process throughout the three-year period. Ms. Di Prima could have sought service through the Hague Convention or sought service by publication before the three-year time limit expired. CCP 538.240(c) does not toll the three-year period because Ms. Oyama's motion to quash service of summons was not filed until August 10, 2016. (Weil and Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (Rutter Group 2015)11: 102-103). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Continued To Nov-29-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Specially Appearing Defendant Yuko Oyama To Quash Service Of Process
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT YUKO OYAMA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Process is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Plaintiff did not file an opposition to this motion. Plaintiff's response to the Order to Show Cause that she filed on September 8, 2016 states that she plans to serve the moving defendant by publication, thereby effectively conceding the merits of this motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
